panels.description: Grid import and Eddi hot-water diversion at 30-min resolution from MyEnergi API. Polled nightly at 23:30. Import mirrors ESB meter data; Eddi gives exact hot-water diversion not available from ESB.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  interval_start AS time,
  import_kwh,
  eddi_kwh
FROM myenergi_readings
WHERE hub_serial = '21509692'
  AND $__timeFilter(interval_start)
ORDER BY interval_start

panels.title: MyEnergi 30-min: Grid Import vs Eddi Diversion

panels.description: Daily totals from MyEnergi. Eddi column = total hot-water diversion that day (includes solar diversion + grid boost).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  TO_CHAR(DATE(interval_start AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin'), 'DD Mon') AS day,
  ROUND(SUM(import_kwh)::NUMERIC, 2)::FLOAT8 AS daily_import_kwh,
  ROUND(SUM(eddi_kwh)::NUMERIC, 2)::FLOAT8 AS daily_eddi_kwh
FROM myenergi_readings
WHERE hub_serial = '21509692'
  AND $__timeFilter(interval_start)
GROUP BY DATE(interval_start AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin')
ORDER BY DATE(interval_start AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin')

panels.title: Daily Import vs Eddi Totals (MyEnergi)

panels.description: How many 30-min samples were collected per day. Full day = 48 samples. Missing samples indicate polling failure or MyEnergi API issue.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  TO_CHAR(DATE(interval_start AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin'), 'DD Mon') AS day,
  COUNT(*)::FLOAT8 AS sample_count,
  48.0::FLOAT8 AS expected_48
FROM myenergi_readings
WHERE hub_serial = '21509692'
  AND $__timeFilter(interval_start)
GROUP BY DATE(interval_start AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin')
ORDER BY DATE(interval_start AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin')

panels.title: Poller Health — Samples per Day (48 = full day)

panels.description: Open-Meteo GHI (Global Horizontal Irradiance) in kWh/m². Forecast written at 20:00 for next day. Actual written at 23:30 from Open-Meteo archive API. Gap between lines = forecast accuracy.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  DATE(hour_utc AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin') AS time,
  ROUND(SUM(ghi_wh_m2) FILTER (WHERE data_type = 'forecast') / 1000.0, 3)::FLOAT8 AS forecast_ghi,
  ROUND(SUM(ghi_wh_m2) FILTER (WHERE data_type = 'actual')   / 1000.0, 3)::FLOAT8 AS actual_ghi
FROM weather_log
WHERE location = 'maynooth'
  AND $__timeFilter(hour_utc)
GROUP BY DATE(hour_utc AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin')
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Daily GHI — Forecast vs Actual (kWh/m²)

panels.description: panel_factor_obs = (export_kwh + eddi_kwh) / ghi_actual. LOWER BOUND — excludes house self-consumption (Harvi CT not installed). Target: ~1.6. Green = 1.0–2.8 (normal). Yellow = 0.5–1.0 (below expected — check ESB data). Red = below 0.5 (data gap). NOTE: panel_factor_obs will remain blank until ESB export data is uploaded via /upload and the daily aggregation job has run.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  solar_date::TIMESTAMPTZ AS time,
  panel_factor_obs::FLOAT8 AS panel_factor_obs,
  1.6::FLOAT8 AS current_model_factor
FROM solar_actuals
WHERE ghi_actual > 0.5
  AND solar_date BETWEEN ($__timeFrom())::date AND ($__timeTo())::date
ORDER BY solar_date

panels.title: Panel Factor (obs vs model) — calibrates morning_advisory.py

panels.description: Daily solar actuals. export_kwh from ESB CSV uploads (populated by daily aggregation from meter_readings); eddi_kwh from MyEnergi API; ghi_actual from Open-Meteo archive. panel_factor_obs = lower bound (excludes house SC). '—' = not yet populated.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  solar_date,
  COALESCE(export_kwh::TEXT, '—') AS export_kwh,
  COALESCE(eddi_kwh::TEXT, '—') AS eddi_kwh,
  COALESCE(ghi_actual::TEXT, '—') AS ghi_actual,
  COALESCE(ghi_forecast::TEXT, '—') AS ghi_forecast,
  COALESCE(panel_factor_obs::TEXT, '—') AS panel_factor_obs
FROM solar_actuals
WHERE solar_date BETWEEN ($__timeFrom())::date AND ($__timeTo())::date
ORDER BY solar_date DESC
LIMIT 30

panels.title: Solar Actuals — Last 30 Days
Data Quality — ESB vs MyEnergi Import Reconciliation (DAN-147)

panels.description: Both ESB (meter_readings) and MyEnergi (myenergi_readings) independently measure grid import at 30-min resolution. Daily sums should be near-identical (±5%). Systematic divergence indicates a timezone mismatch or data quality issue. Difference = ESB minus MyEnergi.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  esb.day AS time,
  esb.daily_import_kwh::FLOAT8 AS "ESB Import (kWh)",
  COALESCE(me.daily_import_kwh, 0)::FLOAT8 AS "MyEnergi Import (kWh)",
  ROUND((esb.daily_import_kwh - COALESCE(me.daily_import_kwh, 0))::NUMERIC, 3)::FLOAT8 AS "Difference (kWh)"
FROM (
  SELECT
    DATE(recorded_at AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin') AS day,
    ROUND(SUM(import_kwh)::NUMERIC, 3) AS daily_import_kwh
  FROM meter_readings
  WHERE household_id = '$household_id'::uuid
    AND $__timeFilter(recorded_at)
  GROUP BY 1
) esb
LEFT JOIN (
  SELECT
    DATE(interval_start AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin') AS day,
    ROUND(SUM(import_kwh)::NUMERIC, 3) AS daily_import_kwh
  FROM myenergi_readings
  WHERE hub_serial = '21509692'
  GROUP BY 1
) me ON me.day = esb.day
ORDER BY esb.day

panels.title: ESB vs MyEnergi Daily Import Reconciliation

panels.description: Percentage difference between ESB and MyEnergi daily import totals. Values near 0% = good agreement. Consistent positive % means ESB reads higher than MyEnergi (expected — ESB is the official meter, MyEnergi measures at the hub which may miss some circuits).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  esb.day AS time,
  CASE WHEN me.daily_import_kwh > 0
    THEN ROUND(((esb.daily_import_kwh - me.daily_import_kwh) / me.daily_import_kwh * 100)::NUMERIC, 1)::FLOAT8
    ELSE NULL
  END AS "Diff % (ESB vs MyEnergi)"
FROM (
  SELECT DATE(recorded_at AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin') AS day,
         ROUND(SUM(import_kwh)::NUMERIC, 3) AS daily_import_kwh
  FROM meter_readings
  WHERE household_id = '$household_id'::uuid
    AND $__timeFilter(recorded_at)
  GROUP BY 1
) esb
JOIN (
  SELECT DATE(interval_start AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin') AS day,
         ROUND(SUM(import_kwh)::NUMERIC, 3) AS daily_import_kwh
  FROM myenergi_readings
  WHERE hub_serial = '21509692'
  GROUP BY 1
) me ON me.day = esb.day
WHERE me.daily_import_kwh > 0.5
ORDER BY esb.day

panels.title: ESB vs MyEnergi Import % Difference (< 5% = good)

panels.description: Summary stats for reconciliation. Mean diff near 0 = good. Consistent positive bias = ESB reads more (normal — MyEnergi hub may only monitor certain circuits). Large outliers = investigate specific dates.

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  ROUND(AVG(ABS(esb.daily_import_kwh - me.daily_import_kwh))::NUMERIC, 3)::FLOAT8 AS mean_diff_kwh,
  ROUND(MAX(ABS(esb.daily_import_kwh - me.daily_import_kwh))::NUMERIC, 3)::FLOAT8 AS max_diff_kwh,
  COUNT(*)::FLOAT8 AS days_compared
FROM (
  SELECT DATE(recorded_at AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin') AS day,
         ROUND(SUM(import_kwh)::NUMERIC, 3) AS daily_import_kwh
  FROM meter_readings
  WHERE household_id = '$household_id'::uuid
    AND $__timeFilter(recorded_at)
  GROUP BY 1
) esb
JOIN (
  SELECT DATE(interval_start AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin') AS day,
         ROUND(SUM(import_kwh)::NUMERIC, 3) AS daily_import_kwh
  FROM myenergi_readings
  WHERE hub_serial = '21509692'
  GROUP BY 1
) me ON me.day = esb.day
WHERE me.daily_import_kwh > 0.5

panels.title: Reconciliation Summary Stats

panels.description: % of total household energy supplied by solar panels each month. Formula: (export_kwh + eddi_kwh) / (import_kwh + export_kwh + eddi_kwh). Requires ESB CSV upload + MyEnergi nightly poll.

targets.rawSql:
WITH monthly AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', solar_date) AS month,
    SUM(export_kwh) AS export_kwh,
    SUM(eddi_kwh)   AS eddi_kwh
  FROM solar_actuals
  WHERE export_kwh IS NOT NULL
    AND eddi_kwh IS NOT NULL
    AND solar_date BETWEEN ($__timeFrom())::date AND ($__timeTo())::date
  GROUP BY 1
),
import_monthly AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', DATE(recorded_at AT TIME ZONE 'Europe/Dublin')) AS month,
    SUM(import_kwh) AS import_kwh
  FROM meter_readings
  WHERE household_id = '$household_id'::uuid
    AND $__timeFilter(recorded_at)
  GROUP BY 1
)
SELECT
  m.month AS time,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * (m.export_kwh + m.eddi_kwh) /
    NULLIF(im.import_kwh + m.export_kwh + m.eddi_kwh, 0)
  , 1)::FLOAT8 AS self_sufficiency_pct
FROM monthly m
JOIN import_monthly im ON m.month = im.month
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Monthly Solar Self-Sufficiency (%)

panels.description: Current month solar self-sufficiency at a glance. Green = ≥30%, yellow = 15–30%, red = <15% (winter baseline).

targets.rawSql:
WITH monthly AS (
  SELECT
    SUM(export_kwh) AS export_kwh,
    SUM(eddi_kwh)   AS eddi_kwh
  FROM solar_actuals
  WHERE solar_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
    AND export_kwh IS NOT NULL
    AND eddi_kwh IS NOT NULL
),
import_monthly AS (
  SELECT SUM(import_kwh) AS import_kwh
  FROM meter_readings
  WHERE household_id = '$household_id'::uuid
    AND recorded_at >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
)
SELECT
  NOW() AS time,
  ROUND(100.0 * (m.export_kwh + m.eddi_kwh) / NULLIF(im.import_kwh + m.export_kwh + m.eddi_kwh, 0), 1)::FLOAT8 AS "Self-Sufficiency (this month)",
  ROUND(m.export_kwh::NUMERIC, 2)::FLOAT8 AS "Export kWh",
  ROUND(m.eddi_kwh::NUMERIC, 2)::FLOAT8 AS "Eddi kWh"
FROM monthly m CROSS JOIN import_monthly im

panels.title: Self-Sufficiency — This Month

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  DATE_TRUNC('month', solar_date)::TIMESTAMPTZ AS time,
  ROUND(AVG((export_kwh + COALESCE(eddi_kwh, 0.0)) / NULLIF(ghi_actual, 0))::NUMERIC, 4)::FLOAT8 AS panel_factor_obs,
  1.6::FLOAT8 AS fallback
FROM solar_actuals
WHERE ghi_actual > 0
  AND export_kwh IS NOT NULL
  AND export_kwh > 0
GROUP BY 1
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 10
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Seasonal Panel Factor — Monthly Average (min 10 days/month)
Eddi Solar Diversion — Forecast vs Actual (DAN-144)

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  al.advisory_date::TIMESTAMPTZ AS time,
  al.expected_diversion_kwh::FLOAT8  AS forecast_diversion_kwh,
  sa.eddi_kwh::FLOAT8                AS actual_eddi_kwh
FROM advisory_log al
LEFT JOIN solar_actuals sa ON sa.solar_date = al.advisory_date
WHERE al.household_id = '$household_id'::uuid
  AND al.advisory_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days'
  AND al.expected_diversion_kwh IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Eddi Solar Diversion — Forecast (dashed) vs Actual (DAN-144)

list.definition: SELECT id FROM households ORDER BY created_at
list.label: Household
list.name: household_id
list.query: SELECT id FROM households ORDER BY created_at
